Empires & Puzzles Jabbar Review: 4-Star Dark Purple Hero Guide

Jabbar is a walking fortress made of sand, but does he have enough grit to stand up to modern heroes? This review explores his niche role as a healing blocker and whether his sturdy stats are enough to justify a spot on your roster.
Jabbar Hero Review: The Sand Golem Who Hates Doctors
Jabbar, The Sandcastle Sentinel: Great Wall of Sand (And Nothing Else)
Jabbar is a 4-star Dark (Purple) hero from the

seasonal event. In his prime, he was considered a sturdy tank option for 4-star tournaments, a "poor man's" defensive anchor who could take a beating. Released years ago, he represents an era where "tankiness" was defined by staying alive rather than counter-attacking with nuclear force. He is a

, which gives him a chance to resist status ailments—a useful trait for a defensive hero. However, in the modern landscape of 2026, Jabbar is a relic of a slower time, standing like an ancient monument that tourists visit but nobody actually lives in.

Jabbar’s design is undeniably cool. He is a massive, animated sand construct with an entire Arabian-style fortress built onto his shoulders and back. He looks like he walked straight out of a fantasy desert epic. The "Brute of Sand Empire" title fits his visual dominance; he occupies the card frame with broad shoulders and a menacing, stony grimace. It’s a shame he doesn't hit as hard as he looks—you’d expect a golem carrying a castle to flatten enemies, not just gently sprinkle sand in their eyes.
Let’s be blunt: Jabbar’s stats are a time capsule.
This is painfully low. For context, modern 4-star healers often have higher attack stats. He hits with the force of a slightly aggressive pillow.
These were his selling points. Even today, they are
for a 4-star, but no longer exceptional. Modern 4-stars like
walk around with nearly 800 Defense and 1500+ Health
emblems. Jabbar’s "tanky" identity has been eroded by power creep.
The skill is a mixed bag of low impact and niche utility.
Direct Damage (235% to target + minor nearby):
With his basement-level attack stat, this scratches the paint. Do not bring him for the damage.
172 damage over 4 turns is negligible in today’s game where boosted health bars exceed 2000 HP. It barely counters passive healing.
A 75% reduction is significant. It nearly shuts down healers like Rigard or Kiril. If you time this right, you can prevent an enemy team from recovering.
A nice survival tool, but since it only affects him, it doesn't help his team much.
Average. He requires 10 tiles to charge (without troops/bonuses). In a world of "Fast" and "Very Fast" slayers, Average speed for this level of impact is slow.
A modern sniper might output a value of 4,000+. Jabbar is not a damage dealer.
The -75% healing is his only true numerical asset.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

bonus gives +10%/+20% Defense for 2/3 heroes. This reinforces his role as a meat shield. If you somehow run him with another Sand hero (like Roc or Rana), he becomes slightly annoying to kill, but that's about it.
Jabbar has no modern passive skills. He is from a simpler time.
When Limit Broken, he gains a chance to counterattack. This actually synergizes well with his high health pool. Since opponents
to hit him eventually, getting some free damage back helps compensate for his wet-noodle arms.

Years ago, Jabbar was a legitimate 4-star tank. In "Field Aid" wars (a now-removed war type that healed teams), his healing reduction was a strategic counter. He was the "Budget Gafar" for players who needed healing control but didn't pull the sniper.
The game has left him in the dust. New 4-star heroes do everything he does but better, faster, and with higher stats. His 531 attack is a liability that is hard to work around.
He is not viable for general play. His only use case is in
if you desperately need a tank and lack better options like Treevil, Franz, or modern event heroes.
Do not try to fix his attack. It is a bottomless pit. Lean into what he is: a brick wall.
Maximizes his survivability. Makes him a tough nut to crack in 4-star tournaments.
His tile damage becomes even more irrelevant.
. You want him to survive long enough to fire his skill once or twice to block enemy healing. If he dies, he contributes nothing.
-75% healing received is potent against heavy healer teams.
Good base HP and Defense for an older 4-star.
Cleanses ailments from himself, making him resistant to DoT or defense down.
Monk class allows him to resist mana control or silence occasionally.
One of the lowest attack stats among 4-star heroes. He poses zero threat via tile damage or direct skill damage.
The healing ailment is uncleansable, which is good, but it only lasts 2 turns, requiring precise timing.
Outclassed by nearly every modern purple 4-star (Cyprian C2, Rigard C2, any new event hero).
His Average speed becomes Very Fast. He can fire early, blocking the enemy from healing up the initial board damage.
Use him if you have no other Dark options for Sand Empire restrictions.
If you need a disposable hero to curse in the early floors.
Jabbar (Tank) flanked by heavy hitters. He absorbs hits and stops the enemy from recovering, while your snipers pick them off.
Tiburtus (Def Down) + Rigard (Att Up) + Jabbar. Tiburtus softens them, Jabbar applies the heal block, and you pray your tiles do the rest.
Don't pair him with other low-damage stall heroes unless you want the match to end in a Time Out (defeat).

If you pull Jabbar early, he can serve as your main tank through the mid-game map stages. His toughness helps him survive boss waves. However, do not prioritize Limit Breaking him. Save your Aethers for

He is a collection piece. Unless you have a specific nostalgia or are doing a "Classic/Old School" challenge, there is no reason to field Jabbar over modern heroes. He is safe to leave at 3/60 or just keep one copy for the museum.
Back in the day, the community was... whelmed.
"He is somewhat underwhelming as a 4-star... Akin to Boomer really."
- A harsh but fair comparison from the archives.
"I doubt anyone will show a +20 Jabbar."
- A forum user predicting the future.
"I consider him one of the worst heroes in the game."
- A sentiment that has only aged like fine wine (or vinegar).
"His HP and D are very solid for a 4-star... he would service as a phenomenal tank."

Jabbar is a hero who brought a shield to a gunfight. He is sturdy, reliable, and fundamentally incapable of hurting anyone. While his healing reduction is a cool mechanic, the duration is too short and his stats are too old to make it count. He’s the sandcastle that the tide of power creep has washed away.
Is Jabbar worth leveling in 2026? A detailed review of the Sand Empire 4-star hero, analyzing his tankiness, healing reduction, and viability in the current meta.
